# Vibe Annotations

> Visual feedback tool for web development. Annotate elements, make design tweaks, and share with AI coding agents via MCP.

## Documentation

- [Overview](https://vibe-annotations.com/docs)
- [Installation](https://vibe-annotations.com/docs/installation)
- [MCP Setup](https://vibe-annotations.com/docs/mcp-setup)
- [Workflows](https://vibe-annotations.com/docs/workflows)
  - [Copy & Paste](https://vibe-annotations.com/docs/workflows/copy-paste)
  - [Multi-Page via MCP](https://vibe-annotations.com/docs/workflows/mcp)
  - [Watch Mode](https://vibe-annotations.com/docs/workflows/watch-mode)
  - [Collaboration](https://vibe-annotations.com/docs/workflows/collaboration)
  - [Agent Annotation](https://vibe-annotations.com/docs/workflows/agents)
- [Architecture](https://vibe-annotations.com/docs/architecture)
- [Troubleshooting](https://vibe-annotations.com/docs/troubleshooting)
- [Release Notes](https://vibe-annotations.com/docs/releases)
- [Terms & Conditions](https://vibe-annotations.com/docs/terms)
- [Contact](https://vibe-annotations.com/docs/contact)

## Quick Start

1. Install browser extension from Chrome Web Store
2. Run: npx vibe-annotations-server init (interactive wizard — installs server, starts it, configures your AI agent across Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Codex, OpenClaw, VS Code)

Manual install (alternative):
- npm install -g vibe-annotations-server
- vibe-annotations-server start
- claude mcp add --scope user --transport http vibe-annotations http://127.0.0.1:3846/mcp

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/RaphaelRegnier/vibe-annotations

- NPM Server: https://www.npmjs.com/package/vibe-annotations-server
